module github.com/dereksantos/cortex

go 1.26.0

require (
	github.com/gofrs/flock v0.13.0

# Project-Bootstrap DAG (`cortex bootstrap`)

## Context

Cortex's `dream.insight` pipeline is reactive — it only fires when the daemon catches idle time, and only on whatever `DreamSource` happens to sample next. A fresh project that has never been "dreamed about" arrives at the REPL with zero indexed context, so turn 1 has nothing useful to retrieve.

This change adds a **project-bootstrap DAG** that runs once on first REPL invocation (and via a standalone `cortex bootstrap` command). It scans the project under principled security rules, samples chunks via a hierarchical fractal sampler, feeds each chunk through an LLM op (A/B between `maintain.extract_insight` and a new `maintain.extract_overview`), and writes `dream.insight` entries to the journal — looping until both coverage signals satisfy the target or the iteration budget is exhausted.

The bootstrap is **language-agnostic by default** (Tier 1: filesystem adjacency + fixed-window line chunking). A `BoundaryAnalyzer` interface leaves room for Tier 2 (regex import detection per language) and Tier 3 (`go/ast`, tree-sitter) without disturbing the controller, sampler, or DAG ops.

from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, Depends
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from .db import get_session
from .models import User, Article
from .schemas import ArticleCreate, ArticleOut

router = APIRouter(prefix="/articles", tags=["articles"])


@router.get("/{article_id}", response_model=ArticleOut)
def get_article(article_id: int, db: Session = Depends(get_session)) -> ArticleOut:
    article = db.query(Article).filter(Article.id == article_id).first()
    if not article:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="article not found")
    return article


@router.post("/", response_model=ArticleOut, status_code=201)
def create_article(
    payload: ArticleCreate,
    db: Session = Depends(get_session),
    user: User = Depends(current_user),
) -> ArticleOut:
    article = Article(
        title=payload.title,
        body=payload.body,
        author_id=user.id,
    )
    db.add(article)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(article)
    return article


@router.delete("/{article_id}", status_code=204)
def delete_article(
    article_id: int,
    db: Session = Depends(get_session),
    user: User = Depends(current_user),
) -> None:
    article = db.query(Article).filter(Article.id == article_id, Article.author_id == user.id).first()
    if not article:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="article not found")
    db.delete(article)
    db.commit()

[project]
name = "articles"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "FastAPI articles service"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = [
    "fastapi>=0.115.0",
    "sqlalchemy>=2.0",
    "pydantic>=2.6",
    "uvicorn[standard]>=0.32",
    "alembic>=1.13",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.0",
    "httpx>=0.27",
    "ruff>=0.7",
    "mypy>=1.13",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py311"

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
addopts = "--strict-markers"

import pytest
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from app.main import app
from app.db import get_session
from tests.conftest import override_session, make_user, make_article


client = TestClient(app)
app.dependency_overrides[get_session] = override_session


def test_get_article_not_found():
    response = client.get("/articles/9999")
    assert response.status_code == 404
    assert response.json()["detail"] == "article not found"


def test_create_article_requires_auth():
    response = client.post("/articles/", json={"title": "t", "body": "b"})
    assert response.status_code == 401


def test_create_article_persists(session):
    user = make_user(session, email="a@b.c")
    with auth_as(user):
        response = client.post("/articles/", json={"title": "Hello", "body": "World"})
    assert response.status_code == 201
    data = response.json()
    assert data["title"] == "Hello"
    assert data["author_id"] == user.id


def test_delete_article_requires_ownership(session):
    owner = make_user(session, email="owner@x.io")
    other = make_user(session, email="other@x.io")
    article = make_article(session, author=owner)
    with auth_as(other):
        response = client.delete(f"/articles/{article.id}")
    assert response.status_code == 404

# Articles

A tiny FastAPI service that demonstrates a CRUD pattern with SQLAlchemy + Alembic + Pydantic v2.

## Why this exists

Use this as the seed for any internal HTTP service that needs:

- request/response schemas separate from ORM models;
- declarative auth via FastAPI's `Depends`;
- a migration story (Alembic) from day one;
- structured logging with `structlog`.

## Quick start

```bash
pip install -e .[dev]
alembic upgrade head
uvicorn app.main:app --reload
```

Navigate to <http://localhost:8000/docs> for the OpenAPI explorer.

## Layout

```
app/
  main.py        — FastAPI app + router wiring
  db.py          — Session factory + Depends provider
  models.py      — ORM classes
  schemas.py     — Pydantic input/output schemas
  handlers.py    — Endpoint handlers
tests/
  test_handlers.py
alembic/
  versions/
```

# Articles UI

A minimal React + TypeScript + Vite front-end for the Articles API.

## Quick start

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Navigate to <http://localhost:5173>. The dev server proxies `/api/*` to <http://localhost:8000>.

## Stack

- React 18 (function components + hooks)
- React Router v6 for routing
- TanStack Query for server state caching
- Zod for runtime schema validation at the API boundary
- Vitest + Testing Library for tests

## Layout

```
src/
  components/   — UI primitives + feature components
  pages/        — Top-level routed pages
  lib/          — API client + shared types (api.ts)
  styles/       — CSS modules
```
